question: Why is the Anti-Comintern Pact significant to the study of WWII Europe?

options.A: It guaranteed Soviet neutrality for the entire war
options.B: It ended the Spanish Civil War
options.C: It signaled the ideological alignment of fascist powers and foreshadowed the Axis alliance
options.D: It created the League of Nations

explanation: It marked early diplomatic alignment of fascist states against communism and pointed toward the later Tripartite (Axis) Pact.
